name: Figs in Cabernet Sauvignon with Almond Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring milk to a boil.
2. Whisk together sugar, egg yolks, and vanilla bean paste in a bowl or mixer until well combined.
3. Gradually add the boiling milk to the sugar and eggs mixture while whisking continuously. Pour this back into a large metal bowl and heat gently over low heat, stirring occasionally with a wooden spatula to prevent curdling. When thickened, remove from heat and cool by placing in an ice water bath.
4. Peel and halve ripe figs, then sprinkle sugar on the cut sides.
5. Place fig halves in a shallow dish along with Cabernet Sauvignon or Port wine. Marinate for at least 2-3 hours, reserving both the figs and liquid.
6. In a saucepan, combine Creme Anglaise, almonds, and pistachio paste. Process in an ice cream machine until smooth.
7. To serve, arrange figs on plates. Form chilled Creme Anglaise into small mounds and place on top of each fig portion. Drizzle reserved marinade over the figs and add whipped topping mixed with sugar. Garnish with chopped almonds and serve.
Source: Great Chefs of San Francisco, Avon Books, 1984
Chef: Udo Nechutnys, The Miramonte Restaurant, San Francisco, CA
